# Configurable Hardware Surfaces

Inference engineers rarely redesign silicon, but they do configure the system
that exposes the silicon.

## Chapter Job

List the hardware and system-level controls an inference engineer can actually
change.

## Configuration Surfaces

- GPU clocks and power limits.
- MIG or partitioning modes where available.
- NUMA pinning.
- CPU affinity.
- Huge pages and memory behavior.
- PCIe and NIC placement.
- Driver, CUDA, ROCm, oneAPI, or vendor runtime versions.
- Container image and kernel module compatibility.
- Precision and quantization choices.
- Tensor parallel and pipeline parallel layout.
- Batch size and scheduler knobs.
- KV cache allocation.
- Model placement and warmup policy.

## Discipline

Every configuration change needs a before/after benchmark and a rollback path.
The book treats configuration as an experimental system, not as a bag of tuning
tips.
